system.name: Oscillator Circuit
system.description: Buffering integrator inside a negative-feedback loop (Modulating + Buffering + Sensing + Inverting). Same topology as the Regulated Buffer, but tuned so the buffer's integration adds the phase lag that turns the converging fixed point into a sustained bounded limit cycle. Demonstrates periodic dynamics emerging purely from primitive composition — the foundation the Lotka-Volterra / SIR demos (#76) stand on.

sources.name: Energy Supply
sources.description: Constant energy input to the oscillator loop

sinks.name: Oscillating Output
sinks.description: Periodically varying energy output from the buffer

subsystems.name: Modulator
subsystems.description: Gates energy inflow by the inverted error signal; loop gain element

subsystems.name: Buffer
subsystems.description: Integrates gated inflow; its storage lag is the phase delay that drives oscillation

subsystems.name: Sensor
subsystems.description: Transduces buffer level to a message signal with moderate gain (tuned high enough to overshoot)

subsystems.name: Inverter
subsystems.description: Computes error = max - sensed level, closing the negative-feedback loop
